insight: During the match between Mexico and South Africa, the hydration breaks seemed to have a neutral effect on both teams' attacking momentum initially, as neither team gained or lost any actions per minute at the first break. However, at the second hydration break, both teams experienced a slight decline in their attacking momentum, with Mexico and South Africa each seeing a decrease of 0.10 actions per minute. This suggests that the breaks may have disrupted the flow of the game, particularly in the second half, but neither team appeared to benefit more than the other in terms of regaining momentum. Overall, the hydration breaks seemed to have a similar, mildly negative impact on both teams' attacking performance, with neither Mexico nor South Africa able to capitalize on the breaks to gain a significant advantage.
